display.purpose: Top-level catalog and detail titles only.

label.purpose: Measurements, dates, and compact technical labels.

shadows.name: popover
shadows.purpose: True overlays such as the account menu only.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main local action with measured terracotta emphasis.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--primary); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--primary); color: white; padding: 10px 15px; font-weight: 680; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--primary-hover); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--focus); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary navigation and reversible utility action.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--border-strong); border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; color: var(--ink); padding: 10px 15px; font-weight: 680; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { border-color: var(--ink); background: var(--surface); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--focus); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.html: <input class="ds-input" placeholder="Search beer, brewery, country, or style">
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; min-height: 44px; border: 1px solid var(--border-strong); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--bg); color: var(--ink); padding: 10px 12px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted); opacity: 1; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--focus); outline: 3px solid color-mix(in oklch, var(--focus) 22%, transparent); outline-offset: 0; }

components.name: Taxonomy Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; border: 1px solid var(--border-strong); border-radius: 999px; background: var(--bg); color: var(--muted); padding: 5px 8px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 620; text-decoration: none; } .ds-chip:hover { border-color: var(--primary); color: var(--ink); } .ds-chip:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--focus); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Score Panel
components.description: Committed-color surface for index, rank, and release context.
components.css: .ds-score { display: grid; min-height: 240px; align-content: space-between; border-radius: 14px; background: var(--accent); color: var(--bg); padding: 24px; } .ds-score strong { font-family: 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 72px; line-height: 1; }

narrative.overview: BearBoard feels like a trusted field guide that has learned how to work on a screen. True white keeps dense catalog data legible. Measured terracotta supplies the human warmth of beer culture, while deep teal gives scores and high-confidence actions a sober data voice.
keyCharacteristics: True-white reading surface with a restrained terracotta accent.
Geist for interface clarity; Geist Mono only for metrics, labels, IDs, and dates.
Dense information organized into tables, definition lists, and clear route hierarchy.
Rounded corners remain compact and functional, never soft or toy-like.
Motion communicates state in 150–250ms and disappears under reduced motion.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is used for action and evidence emphasis only. If more than one tenth of a product screen is terracotta, the interface has become decoration.

rules.name: The Metric Voice Rule
rules.body: Geist Mono is reserved for values that benefit from stable width or technical distinction.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Content panels never use ambient shadows.

dos: Do keep body text at WCAG 2.2 AA or better.
Do render missing measurements as Not reported.
Do pair every visualization with exact accessible values.
donts: Don't build faux-scientific interfaces with tiny labels, unexplained jargon, or manufactured certainty.
Don't use brewery-poster nostalgia, distressed textures, or ornamental heritage styling.
Don't create generic AI dashboards from repeated floating cards, gradients, oversized hero metrics, or decorative motion.
Don't present fictional demonstration data as verified fact.
